sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: S&P Global은 2025년 4분기 매출 39억 1,600만 달러를 기록하며 Ratings와 Indices 부문의 성장에 힘입어 연간 실적 호조를 달성했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 2025년 4분기 매출은 전년 동기 대비 9% 증가한 39억 1,600만 달러를 기록했으며, GAAP 희석 주당순이익(EPS)은 32% 증가한 3.75달러를 달성했습니다.

businessChanges.text: Ratings 부문은 거래 및 비거래 수익의 두 자릿수 성장에 힘입어 12%의 매출 증가를 기록했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025년 연간 조정 주당순이익은 전년 대비 14% 증가한 17.83달러를 기록했습니다.

watchItems.text: 2026년 가이던스로 제시된 유기적 고정환율 매출 성장률 6.0%~8.0% 및 주당순이익 19.40달러~19.65달러 달성 여부.
2026년 중반으로 예정된 Mobility 사업부 분사(Spin-off) 완료에 따른 GAAP 재무 가이던스 변화.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: www.spglobal.com Press Release For Immediate Release S&P Global Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results New York, NY, February 10, 2026 – S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI) today reported fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results. This earnings release and supplemental materials are available at http://investor.spglobal.com/Quarterly-Earnings. The Company reported fourth quarter 2025 reported revenue of $3.916 billion, an increase of 9% compared to the fourth quarter of 2024. Fourth quarter GAAP net income increased 29% to $1,134 million and GAAP diluted earnings per share increased 32% to $3.75 driven primarily by strong revenue and operating profit growth in Ratings and Indices. Adjusted net income for the fourth quarter increased 12% to $1.299 bi  Normalized numeric aliases: $3.916 billion = 39억 1,600만 달러; $1,134 million = 11억 3,400만 달러; $1.299 billion = 12억 9,900만 달러; $3.916 = 3.916달러; $1,134 = 1,134달러; $3.75 = 3.75달러; $1.299 = 1.299달러; $4.30 = 4.30달러; 1,134 million = 11억 3,400만 달러.

$15.336 billion. 2025 GAAP net income increased 16% to $4.471 billion and GAAP diluted earnings per share increased 19% to $14.66, driven primarily by revenue and operating profit growth in the Company's Ratings, Indices, and Market Intelligence divisions. 2025 adjusted net income increased 11% to $5.441 billion compared to 2024 adjusted net income, and adjusted diluted earnings per share increased 14% to $17.83 compared to 2024 adjusted diluted earnings per share. • S&P Global delivered revenue growth of 8% y/y in 2025. GAAP operating margin improved by approximately 3 percentage points y/y, and adjusted operating margin increased 140 basis points. • G  Normalized numeric aliases: $15.336 billion = 153억 3,600만 달러; $4.471 billion = 44억 7,100만 달러; $5.441 billion = 54억 4,100만 달러; $6.2 billion = 62억 달러; $1.2 billion = 12억 달러; $5.0 billion = 50억 달러; $15.336 = 15.336달러; $4.471 = 4.471달러; $5.441 = 5.441달러; 140 basis points = 140포인트; $17.83 = 17.83달러; $6.2 = 6.2달러; $1.2 = 1.2달러; $5.0 = 5.0달러.

is represents 113% of adjusted free cash flow for the year. • 2026 adjusted guidance calls for organic constant currency revenue growth of 6.0% to 8.0%, and diluted EPS in the range of $19.40 to $19.65, GAAP guidance to be provided upon completion of the Mobility spin (expected mid-2026). “We delivered a strong quarter driven by performance in all divisions, momentum in private markets, and expansion with our CCO clients. I'm very proud of what we accomplished in 2025. We outlined our new mission of Advancing Essential Intelligence, and our medium-term strategy designed to drive durable, profitable growth. Our unified leadership team and the talent of our people enabled us to execute very well. The scale of innovation and pace of AI integration in our products and internal processes was a leap forward for our clients and the business. Our strategy, coupled with the resilience of our busi  Normalized numeric aliases: $19.40 = 19.40달러; $19.65 = 19.65달러.

ness model, and favorable secular and cyclical tailwinds, position us well to deliver the vision and financial targets we established at our Investor Day in November." Martina Cheung President and CEO Page 1 Fourth Quarter 2025 Revenue Fourth quarter revenue increased 9%, driven by strong growth in all divisions. Revenue from subscription products increased 8%. (1) Total revenue for the fourth quarter 2024 and the fourth quarter 2025 includes the impact of inter-segment eliminations of $48M and $53M, respectively. Market Intelligence revenue increased 7%, driven by strong organic subscription revenue growth and the acquisition of With Intelligence in November 2025, partly offset by slower growth in volume-driven products and year-over-year declines in non-subscription revenue. Ratings revenue increased 12%, driven by double-digit transaction and non-transaction revenue growth. Transactio  Normalized numeric aliases: $48 = 48달러; $53 = 53달러.
